**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:30:44` **forthebux** said:

Storage is getting cheaper, compute tends to be where the cost sits - so there are really two things at play here - only you having your data, and then doing something meaningful/useful with it - inc something like content delivery. For me the big gap was the payments for artists and the privacy (or not) for fans. One should be direct (payments) and one should be opt in. But in short yes - I see huge scope in nodes running decentralised (not hosted on centralised servers) and for people building those apps/products/store fronts (or whatever else) on them to benefit from savings, and for producers not needing to have folks in the middle and for fans/users being able to choose what they do and don't share. Music wise, Napster kinda went this route to start with ... kind of, Im pretty close to those guys also.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1212874624447291393)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:31:31` **forthebux** said:

SAFE is truly decentralized and that means no contracts, gatekeepers or continual payment for services. SAFE is the only network, we know of, that runs on commodity hardware and includes everyone from the ground level upwards. Aslo not every person using Safe is going to running Nodes.  Maybe 5 or 10% will, it would be nice to think more would, but take a young family, typically there will be one device running nodes if any.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `20:53:30` **forthebux** said:

SAFE is truly decentralized and that means no contracts, gatekeepers or continual payment for services. SAFE is the only network, we know of, that runs on commodity hardware and includes everyone from the ground level upwards. The network operates through what is known as stigmergy; a fascinating strand of both biology, sociology, and computer science.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1214314803900981368)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1214314911128092742)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `20:54:23` **forthebux** said:

With SAFE:

You can create an account and login without any other human or entity in the chain - it is completely private

All data is encrypted by default (unlike IPFS) and stored indefinitely

Hardware requirements for nodes are low, so centralized hosting is not required to participate - meaning the network stays truly decentralized

The infrastructure allows for the personalization of AI models, enabling users to upload their private data for tailoring and customization

You can build with any AI model you wish, the network is also hardware agnostic; meaning your model will be on any machine you are on and nothing can prevent you from getting access to it

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1214315027226689546)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:05:42` **forthebux** said:

Short answer; yes, relevant aspects are factored into design.

The nature of what the network will do versus what's built on top of it as an experience or service layer is important to flag though context wise. The white paper will be a must read - there will also be a pre-read for community, so that we can gather and answer questions ahead of wider release/promotion.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `20:41:15` **forthebux** said:

It's a big topic (and one that I can't pretend to have a qualified answer to), all I would say is, if you really want something (and you're fortunate enough to be in a society that isn't adversarial toward you - no matter who the 'who' is), then you will try and make it happen - sure maybe you'll fall on your face, maybe you'll fall short, maybe you will just fall out of love with the original idea or vision - but if you choose to keep on reaching and you keep on falling - eventually you'll be able to stand up stronger and move faster, whoever you are.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `07:36:01` **forthebux** said:

It’s not recorded as we would like to encourage people outside of the team to speak, a fair few folk find it easier when they know they’re not going to be recorded/have their words played back. It’s not the only format though - spaces every other week will be recorded and are designed for a broader audience -  and hopefully the conversations flowing in here and on the forum, as well as news updates, and for newcomers aspects such as the faqs will keep things rolling nicely 🤝

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `03:12:09` **jamsplayer** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Please don't judge a very simple landing page, that's temporary 🙏 This brand has so much depth and t... [truncated]

I think the most recent twitter logo was created in a similar way and was the best looking by far. X was quite a rebrand but seems to be part of a bigger picture (or obsession) by Musk. People griped about X rebrand, and TradingView rebrand, but everyone has gotten over it and still use those services everyday, probably more now more than before.

The depth in the branding is already apparent to me. In the short branding video the circles (maybe nodes? Maybe us? Maybe tokens? Maybe all of the above!) would move to the words displayed. With “Earning” the dots grew, “Spending” the dots shrank, “Living” the dots pulsed like a heart beat, “Playing” the dots spun. That is a level of detail in itself that shows how much thought goes into the messaging, verbal and visual cues. 

It seems these circles play a representative role in the branding.

And no one should underestimate the creative genius of Jim! That man tells stories and creates landscapes.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1220208303045607484)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:58:04` **forthebux** said:

**Q26. I suspect that people will want to be able to buy tokens for services or goods rather than exclusively getting tokens by providing resources. How do you foresee the relationship between tokens and fiat currencies, and do you have any plans to avoid the extreme volatility in relation to fiat currencies of blockchain coins like Bitcoin?**

Overview answer to convey:

I'm sure people will want to use the token for a range of things - that we can't even foresee at the moment!

But - ultimately, there is a utility to it, data and communications. And (I think) that will be the relationship between the token and fiat, its relationship to the cost of those services, and the cost to nodes to provide them.

That said - it's an autonomous system, that connects to a complex open system of the Global economy. So we can't eliminate volatility, nor should we. Hopefully though, it should be a bit smoother over the long term because of that relationship to data.

Also good to note - users need not touch FIAT at all if they don't want to… it can function like it's own barter economy… I can offer my spare local resources to offset my costs of storing data on the network. So the FIAT volatility becomes a lot less relevant there.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1222303587997323324)

## Tuesday, 2024-04-02

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `01:01:43` **forthebux** said:

Yes- we will likely need to move toward a more live service model for developers and builders 🤝 - whether that’s best on discord is another matter of course, but certainly can be a portal for support if nothing else

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1224509031868006520)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `17:02:20` **forthebux** said:

it's funny, I have such an allergic reaction to digital identity and centralised digitised currency - as they want the opposite to what we want - access to our data, to inform decisions about our access (perhaps im too cynical ...). That said we would be the perfect platform for seed phrases at a personal level - and unhackable data at an organisation level

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1224750779722891435)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:38:36` **mightyfool0689** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> it's funny, I have such an allergic reaction to digital identity and centralised digitised currency ... [truncated]

I feel the same way, but they're actually about to do quite the opposite. They're looking to extract data from a central source (like ID from the government) and put it in a decentralized, use owned, digital wallet. That way, when an exchange for example needs to know you're a human over 18 year, they can ask for the users ID and if over 18. They'll simply get a "yes" in return for over 18, as that's all they need to know. I really think Autonomi will be great for the purpose and getting in these conversations that banks and governments are having right now might be great to help push laws towards something that could make the network a useful tool for it and benefit the network adoption.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1224820304040300654)

---

## Monday, 2024-04-22

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `13:15:27` **forthebux** said:

I also don't think 'like', 'playlists', 'mood' and curation in general need to be exclusive to centralized apps -  just you're more likely to know exactly what the same do and don't have/know about you - that said I'm more interested in the new models of music experience (fan and artists) than I am in the duplication of the current experience on a truly decentralized platform (although in fairness that is still also a big win)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1231941437923397665)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `14:38:43` **Deleted User** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> I also don't think 'like', 'playlists', 'mood' and curation in general need to be exclusive to centr... [truncated]

I'd rather napster than anything that spoon feeds me what some algorithm decides, unless i get to control the algorithm which is one of those things I think an LLM would be useful and safe for.

Having said that, I no longer spend much time listening to or seeking out music so I'm nobody's demographic atm. But maybe @JamsPlayer can bring me back. He won't need to do much tbh, I'll be using it for fun, and to support such a great guy.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:06:26` **forthebux** said:

That is still the name of the company building the network. SAFE the network name has evolved into Autonomi. A couple of drivers there, not everyone is as positive about the history/timeline as others, there is a SAFE coin on CEX which could cause some confusion for folk, the network has evolved while it still most definitely represent ‘Secure Access For Everyone’ - it is also autonomous in a positive way and in being so protects our knowledge, rights, privacy and control (our autonomy). You don’t have to love the name of course, but hopefully you’ll be pleased with the tech and hopefully the future that can be built on top of it by many many people. @DCrypto

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1249137495434727495)

 I have seen the roadmap and checked it again just too be sure it hadn't been updated or I didn't check it properly enough.  My question was probably also a bit too much all over the place, what I was mostly after is a guided effort to create sort of an ongoing cooperative open commons type learn and building together environment. Perhaps more a category of channels or server where well indented individuals can gather and collaborative open source efforts can emerge from. Maybe even combined with an iterative process to improve that environment itself by the participants (and to slowly take ownership of it). Of course this can be set up by community members themselves also and I understand this would require time and effort from the team, which is in high demand for various efforts, so was just wondering if such a thing had been given any thought.

I spent 1,700 today uploading four chunks, all hard earned donations. I have had 700 more gifted since, but I probably need four or five times that just to start. > Indeed! I know the brave guys pretty well … 😉

Brave have done IPFS integration. I tried it once and it was a terrible UX.

Integration with Autonomi would be easy for them to do and the UX seamless. I've even written the code they would need. 😉 

I looked to build my demo using existing browsers but they are all enormous and complex. It would have taken me too long just to figure out how to integrate, and probably much more onerous to build and test, let alone implement.

So my demo is bare bones, but includes publishing using the same app.

If only I had a few thousand more nanos to upload the resources for showing it off, you could demo it to them. 😆

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:17:42` **forthebux** said:

No, we use a 7 day total, so while all the amounts will cumulatively increase, it’s the 7 day period of earnings (from nodes and boosts), that will give you a rank and therefore really matter, if you earn nothing more than your initial balance, as long as you earn more than a nano (once we’ve data sorted), then while you will get something for running, you won’t be in a high position to earn a greater proportion of the prize pool compared to those who are actively running/hosting and earning weekly boosts

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1253398385764077619)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:08:31` **rid._.dim** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> We will keep an eye on the boost system and will be introducing other variants which will favour and... [truncated]

Actually I personally think additional variants of boosts will make all kinds of leader board participants more frustrated 😉

If you keep changing the rules it's just super difficult to know what one should do....
.... Imho cleaner and clearer would be separate pots for different purposes.... One for node running (linear distribution - just like it will be on the live network - the way we're doing it now and skewed by the boosts is far far away from the final economics)

Then a pot for referrals and one for development or so.... Mixing it all up makes it super hard to know the rules of game and playing it right...

.... And with first referrals splitting up 50% of all token earned makes later use of first referrals ever more powerful and I doubt I'll ever end up in the top 10 again if additional boosts get added to the mix.... Which is kind of okay... But not what I expected of the preliminary node running to test the network economics (which are not really tested anymore if we're honest with ourselves....)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1256355576557076642)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:16:07` **forthebux** said:

Understood - we will publish the referral boost schedule on Monday as a table so it is super clear for folks. I respect that a lot of people have been here, and putting an awful lot into this for a very long time. I respect that not everyone will like the calls I make, and while there will be some that will be wrong/will need changing, there will be others that have a good impact for the network.

There was a real skepticism when I landed about this being real this time, a lot of disillusioned contributors and funders -  and some very upset people for even me starting a discord - there likely still are. 

But being scared of upsetting people, or not pushing to prevent mistakes is likely going to damage the same people it is designed to protect - in the long term at least. A few hundred people participating in a network is admiral and important but it's not going to move the needle in any real way. 

This is an engagement campaign and engagement is the key for all those wanting to host to be able to do (and earn) from doing just that - look at how the flow of nanos effects things (esp sentiment) even now.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1256357489037807847)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:00:36` **forthebux** said:

First and foremost appreciate your candidness, as always. I do object a little to the framing as there are no sales involved here - and there is also in my opinion, very, very little chance, reason or opportunity right now for 'the masses' to be part of Autonomi. 

There is no getting away from the fact that this is a token based network - payments need to be made in exchange for services, whether that be node operators providing them, or users uploading (data) and interacting with applications on the network paying for them. 

To frame this slightly differently -I have spent most of my working life (22 years), not in web3, but often working with large business and big brands who put (in theory) CX and UX above all else. But, we simply are not there yet - and while I appreciate we could just wait (which perhaps is the preference of some here and maybe the tendency of some of the MaidSafe team) for things to be right and fully ready, the world doesn't wait - it also doesn't react instantly and spontaneously to new networks or businesses, no matter what purpose is driving them. 

My role, while I'm at MaidSafe for next few months, is to launch a successful network, the definition of success can be a multi faceted one, some would say a network live with a few hundred people using it and trying to build on it (like the early days of the Internet, but of course which now exists in its full form) but I do not believe this type of deployment would come that close to  fulfilling the promises and expectations people have developed (and been set) over many years. What is motivation to build something in a place where there are no users? What provides the ability for node operators to be paid for their efforts if there's no inherent value of the token they're receiving? How can we change the world of big tech, if we're not reaching people to do it?

The reality is we currently only have 175 people (who were in wave 1 ahead of boosts or anything else), who up until Thursday, have been offered the chance to invite one person into the beta and into a different wave. Any judgement of the contribution of those in Wave 1 is currently unfair, due to issues the network side, but the merit of their contribution will been seen over weeks of activity.

*the network will be invisible to most people, the applications we're able to launch on it, will not be. There is little point in me trying to engage those who need the layer we don't yet have, but I appreciate it will take us a little learning to find the right folks who want to host and support the network, as opposed to harvest and dump a token - pleasebe assured we are accutely aware of that, trying to find the balance, and trying to find the right communities to engage with, which is actually why starting with the current community (referrals) at very small numbers 1-3 people seemed to be a good route to try

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1256610277844320326)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:30:05` **forthebux** said:

It does, as we're going through testing we wanted to make sure those helping received token allocation for their contributions - the way it works is that the nanos earned in a 7 day period (12.01pm GMT+1 Friday to 12.00pm GMT+1 the following Friday), give you a rank on a leaderboard (this is made up of people in the same wave), and a prize pool allocation is then shared among them, based on their rank

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:28:13` **forthebux** said:

that's Peca - @Sam69 the guys shared this with me as well:

Before any data is uploaded on the Network, it is split into small chunks that are encrypted and meaningless on their own. Those are then sent to different addresses, meaning different people on the network. A small chunk of this data on its own is meaningless and in some rare cases could also end up being a part of another piece of data that could share this chunk.

So nobody is holding all the chunks of the BAD data except for the uploader. The farmers only hold it in small pieces of encrypted meaningless data. A node holding one chunk won't be able to restore the entire file.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1258081877026406570)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:33:35` **dimitarsafenetworkbulgaria** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> that's Peca - @Sam69 the guys shared this with me as well:

Before any data is uploaded on the Netwo... [truncated]

That last one means that the network is resistant to quantum computers, even with them you can't break the encryption without knowing all the pieces of data that make up a file

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1258083227701215362)

I think the expression is ‘bus mans’ holiday … I’ve been taking a little time to work on what’s going to be on the network, while the guys make sure it’s able to handle all the data I’d like us all and a few specific folk to throw at it

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1271197469610344499)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:05:45` **forthebux** said:

Did you know that at one stage we had 80k nodes running … (needed that for a deck to prove we’ve been all about iteration through testnets)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1271197684174163970)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:09:36` **forthebux** said:

Here’s some more stuff from @JimCollinson … you might like to know …

While we’ve dropped off that number now (80k), that’s not necessarily because there are fewer nodes participating, but because the network is being more strict on shunning slower/underperforming nodes, with the aim of maximising performance …

*Amount of data hosted*
- Current storage hosted 5TB
- Raw capacity 32TB
- Average connected peers for each node: *280*

That’s each node communicating with and checking the work of 280 other nodes. Those little ants! Go team!

We’ve also now had 4 releases for the network itself, and a bunch of others for things like the launchpad and a whole string of optimisations improving the health of the network and dialling in performance (and gathering experiment results at scale) release by release.

E.g…

- Network traffic for nodes is down by 10-15% at last release, and around 25% overall. That means the network is operating more efficently and we are improving it each time.
- Upload speed improved by 50%

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1271198650856378571)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `23:21:59` **janeytheterrible** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Here’s some more stuff from @JimCollinson … you might like to know …

While we’ve dropped off that n... [truncated]

_"While we’ve dropped off that number now (80k), that’s not necessarily because there are fewer nodes participating, but because the network is being more strict on shunning slower/underperforming nodes, with the aim of maximising performance …"_

Another world view is that it is more to do with a problem with people who are using the launchpad, as @Dimitar pointed out that of the people he is helping, none who are running launcher (>20) have had success, I tested it and its BAD, the nodes run but are not communicating.  And for the last week we only hear of people not earning when using launchpad

There is the major reason for drop in nodes.  Not bad detection, or they are completely rejected with no shun messages being sent and/or received.  Vdash showed zero PUTs/GETs, zero shun messages.  But the nodes are running, but obviously not participating.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1271231969451311239)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `17:59:17` **forthebux** said:

A maker of a USB or indeed a hard drive cannot know what someone stores on it. If we want to know everything about everyone then we cannot have privacy or security like we used to have when things were localised. Applications have agreements with their users, but their users are not in the driving seat when it comes to what they can keep and what they want to share. Just look at meta getting busted for using biometric data that most won't even have thought about FB taking based on logins etc.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1271513145885589556)

## Monday, 2024-08-12

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `17:22:24` **forthebux** said:

Hey @Raúl thanks for ping! Overall (inc partners etc) we're looking good. We've adjusted a little in terms of announcements and onboarding/data, but think we're in an ever stronger position for October, and folks to want to start talking about and sharing it - which of course means scale (important for all). I've been doing a lot on partnership aspects over last few weeks (gateway etc), as always, lots more to do but do think it's fair to say that I don't think people are expecting what we have in store which is a blessing as far as im concerned 😉

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1272591025197682820)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:47:58` **forthebux** said:

I'm aware, know you guys see github/code releases etc. My job is to make sure Im tackling the issues **and** that the sequencing is done in such a way that components come online in the right order and that key aspects (disaster recovery for example) are present when they do. **[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:53:44` **forthebux** said:

personal use case will only come with time - a crazy startegy (imo) is trying to launch (and more specifically scale) a network on a 1:1 basis when there are many, many alternative (less compelling in some senses but way more in others) out there. For a network to thrive it needs to be used, it needs data and while I accept that can come from individuals over time, it will not be enough for the first few years to create the demand, visibility and experience/use case that will keep the nodes coming online and the rewards for the same flowing. What the network hosts will be what gets the network attention (and therefore scale) - imo.

'ordinary' folk aren't thinking about hosting the internet (maybe one day, and that's the win for those who are early) but they are very happy using it, we need to play into what people (and ventures) are doing to start with, we cannot flick a switch, we have to instigate a change - perhaps one people don't even need to think about too much.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1272614010767937617)

---

## Tuesday, 2024-08-13

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:42:57` **janeytheterrible** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> So, the issue there is that we have had one main issue being recorded (lack of nanos), which I would... [truncated]

I did a longer test on using launchpad (linux) for 24 hours.  Took me time starting the 40 nodes, after some 6 hours 15 nodes had received any PUTs and vdash shoed connected, the other 25 just had zero PUTs/GETs and status of started.  One node had a store cost showing.  

After 24 hours 25 of the 40 had any PUTs/GETs and a few had store costs.  ZERO had any earnings  and 15 had ZERO PUTs/GETs w/ status of started 

I reset and closed down launchpad and use safenode-manager with my port forwards to start the 40 nodes with 3 minutes gap between starting.

All were connected with PUTs/GETs within seconds of each starting.  Store quotes were appearing within a short time and first earnings in less than 6 hours.  By 18 hours the 40 had earned 240 nanos (24 of the 40 earned 10 nanos)

This is a stark contrast of using relays compared to port-forwarding.
Prior to the last update home-nodes were earning like port-forwarding (I have 2 ISP connections) just slightly slower.
@JimCollinson You might like to see my update to launchpad ^^^

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1272701893612798085)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `19:59:12` **forthebux** said:

When you join the network, you won't be holding any data. You will receive replication request from the nodes around you very quickly though. Replication requests means other nodes are telling you that you need to hold certain data chunks based on your network address.

Until you replicate the data you are required to hold, uploaders won't ask your node to generate quotes or pay your node. 

So ,if you don't have storage space to hold the data your nodes are currently required to hold, you won't get paid.

Furthermore, if you initially fulfil all your storage duty but stop doing so later, you will be shunned by other nodes and you won't receive any payment as your node won't be discoverable by the uploader.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1291837045211271190)

## Friday, 2024-11-29

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `19:08:20` **forthebux** said:

hello you, what a lovely message, thank you so very much 💗

The good news is you guys have been and will always remain way ahead of me - which is also sad news, as it appears you will never require any of my stellar coding skills nor my technical-prowess ... 😉

Being candid, I'm here to help clear the last few yards (metres) with/for you all. To make sure the people who invested in Maidsafe realise something amazing (network included!), and so that token holders feel not just inspired but bullish about what we're (soon) releasing, meaning the network will continue to thrive and be supported by nodes long after we're all gone. I'm also here to make sure, that builders, pioneers and ventures who actually 'get it' can finally start creating the internet that we (and web3 believers and dreamers) deserve. 

Here's to the next few weeks of crazy, to the many years that have been spent to get to them, and to the huge opportunities that await us all after them.

Grateful to have (finally!) joined the journey!

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1312133064637546529)

## Friday, 2024-12-06

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `01:29:29` **forthebux** said:

Based on feedback from the WP release in March - and looking at the tokens out (which will be 1:1) in the market, plus the payment/reward/incentive design and the need for longevity/token stability, it felt that it was sensible to reduce supply rather than have years of significant emissions (inflationary) and substantive questions/concerns around the foundation 'control' of a very large pool of funds.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1314403310874132591)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `01:30:20` **forthebux** said:

I also ask Jae to react to this directly and his response (which always sounds better ...) was/is:

*Our analysis led us to make a conclusion that we only need emission to sustain during the initial period. If we are to focus the emission on the short period of time (~12 years instead of 100+ years), we don't need as many tokens. At the same time, the reduction in total emission amount will significantly reduce the uncertainty and improve the trust.*

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1314403523521155112)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `01:34:09` **forthebux** said:

The emissions have been tweaked further - @DavidMc0 gave some super inputs there - he might be sad they're not removed all together but feel like we got to a good place after **a lot** of hammering on it

Bridge is centralized solution, requires trusted entity hodling 400 million tokens. It can be stolen, it can be hacked. It can be attacked and bring legal risks. And does not bring forever duration. Airdrop allows OMNi holders to claim token in the future. No risk of missing deadlines... not talking on how very expensive are OMNI transactions. Now people need to buy BTC, sent it to omni and than pay transaction in omni to burn it. It is such a bad solution. I would rather see network a postoponed with airdrop than earlier with bridge.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:10:56` **forthebux** said:

Yes - the guys had been working on the OMNI -network bridge, as part of development. We will come back to that, along with the Native token work (I was given an 18 month timeline for the native token, but it needs to be better interrogated and ofc then communicated). 

The current focus is to get the network live, with all the needed and expected (storage) fundamentals in place  - this is captured in WP (but noting here that other documentation in Gitbook needs to be brought up to date). 

For initial phase we will use an ERC20 token for payments and rewards/incentives (ANT) - airdropping to the same to eth wallets 1:1 at TGE, for both EMAID and any earned 'test tokens'. Also digging into Dimitar's suggestion and steer re: easy exchange of OMNI $MAID to ANT (also 1:1) @SoHaKun

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `23:30:01` **forthebux** said:

We will have to build up to those - with some volume and exposure. CEXs like Binance are always happy to see WP and proposals but those guys won’t pre agree anything, they inform if/when they want to make a listing - and to do that will look more at a projects coverage, holder/follower numbers and the market trading demand - as we’ve seen with the memes coins. That said, MAID was top flight back in the day, and there’s a good amount to get excited about again with the network now launching 😊

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1315376778902114455)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `17:59:30` **forthebux** said:

The guys hammered on websockets and WASM stuff for ages - but the fundamental role of this network produced app was to test the network by giving folk access to it via GUI to try the file upload/download and payment aspects

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1315377233417732149)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:00:10` **forthebux** said:

The scope is:

**Upload Data:**: Provide access to the local file system for uploading files.
**Payment Integration:** Utilize WalletConnect for users to pay for uploads.
**File Management:** Simulate a folder structure to organize files.
**Download Files:** Enable users to download files as needed.
**Authentication: **Support Ethereum wallet authentication

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1315377398124118016)

## Friday, 2024-12-20

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `11:39:41` **forthebux** said:

We will ensure that there is enough incentive for nodes to run and be rewarded, as we move from an empty network with test data (as we are today), to a permanent network with useful public data (from Maidsafe uploaders) and private uploaded data. We won't just turn uploaders (and by extension the incentives attached to them) off - we have to get things started, until the data flows are great enough that we can take our hands off the wheel

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `13:15:32` **forthebux** said:

You’re right, lots of wallets with tiny amounts won’t do much at all. The write is on way, but headline is:

- Uploader reward: aims to distribute rewards evenly based on the number of nodes you are running

- Random rewards: aims to add an aspect of surprise and unpredictability for outreach as well as incentive  

We know people can still ‘game’ (whether through running large amounts nodes or setting up new wallets), but both these actions enhance and support the ecosystem - so it’s a win-win anyway - and with the price to enter able to be zero, I don’t see that anyone, minus disappointed of not getting a random reward across 20 days of them, is losing (noting they will still get uploader rewards).

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1319654423684845578)

Yep, staking for one benefits only the rich, and disadvantages the poor.  The opposite of the goals of the network to benefit the world as a whole. ## Saturday, 2024-12-21

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:54:03` **forthebux** said:

Exactly, it’s a decentralised network, that is run by everyone and owned by no one - thus the transfer of maidsafe from private ownership (needed to build the network and for shareholder and token holder rewards for being able to do the same), to a non profit foundation. 

To be clear - We are not debating network fundamentals here - those are set and crystal clear - ‘control’ won’t be with any group or with an executive committee, and to be candid, I’m not sure why that would be an interpretation when talking about token holder value or application builder benefits, which is the topic to which my DAO comment relates.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1319830209352040468)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `01:06:24` **dimitarsafenetworkbulgaria** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Exactly, it’s a decentralised network, that is run by everyone and owned by no one - thus the transf... [truncated]

Most people here are captivated by the idea of ​​Autonomi and have no experience in testing other products, respectively, they have no experience with DAOs and it is normal to think the worst. Crypto is full of projects that offer staking for a certain period in exchange for a reward, which locks some of the tokens out of the market. These locks and the rewards for them are often resolved through DAOs.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1319833316681318481)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `01:18:24` **forthebux** said:

Understand, a good reminder that sharing early isn’t always the right route!  I should have waited until NY to provide all details, just felt it was important for people to know that we understand how impactful incentives can be on scale and appeal for builders and other participants (particularly when network is in early phase, with little experiences/apps to attract or interact with) - looking after these elements doesn’t need to (and won’t) affect network fundamentals. I don’t feel the WP compromises these for example  (nothing has changed - beyond the payment tech at launch  - with the network, 4 data types, one time payment, instant node rewards etc etc). Will do a proper write up but hopefully my previous message alleviates some of the reactionary concerns 🙏

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `13:24:27` **redmax92** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> (Autonomi could also be the permanent storage layer, a bit of compare and contrast would be needed w... [truncated]

Exactly, that’s what I was thinking. The edge storage beta is supposed to be available soon-ish, like start of 2025, waiting on the details here. Here’s what we know so far: https://wiki.edge.network/getting-started/edge-products/object-storage 
So there is some overlap with autonomi in concepts, but edge is targeted more towards performant, low latency solutions and less long-term storage & completely autonomous solutions.
So if there’d be an option to store files on autonomi longterm but have fast distribution to the point of need through caching in edge host and gateway nodes, that’d be super cool.

## Saturday, 2025-01-11

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:41:06` **forthebux** said:

Understood @happybeing thanks for raising, super aware of concerns, I'm sure the team are trying their very best to communicate - just a huge amount to get through, so at times it's hard for them to keep up with everything, including some of the (perhaps fair) expectations. Uploads and downloads - as well as some supporting econ testing are absolutely top of mind and with API progress will be much easy to test far and wide, we all agree that is vital. 

That said, Chris, David, Anselme, Qi and Roland, are likely more active in forum than here, although I know you have raised this there too - so beyond that all I can say is they're aware and on it, and confident with David, that the same is within the needed timeframe to help build further (including new) enthusiasm and support for the project, which is a key role for this discord too.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1327754185009266801)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `12:28:36` **forthebux** said:

I’m not sure what particular message(s) this is based on and/or who it is aimed at specifically (if anyone) - but please note, I’m now very careful not to post in the forum as appreciate my ‘style’ and role (that I was hired in to do as it was believed that it was very much needed) - doesn’t perhaps sit well/feel right for some key folks there. I acknowledge that it’s more of a technical space and a place for developers to kick ideas back and forth and for those who have been in the project a long time to continue with their relationships with others who know them. 

Discord was/is a new space and the messaging and/or announcements here have and will continue to try to walk the line between sharing openly and building excitement (which is needed for growth, noting my previous comment here re: my absence as a way of respecting and not changing dynamics in the forum) … while not sharing too early or at too high a level to cause frustration/disappointment in terms of needed changes or lack of clarity. Clearly with something this challenging and as a very much tech/engineer-led project, changes in plans cannot always be avoided.

Talking of avoid, I’m super okay (and incidentally it wouldn’t matter if I wasn’t and rightly so) with criticism and concerns being voiced, yours have been heard, and concerns re: the features that are lacking testing and completion for public use are (as you might expect) also top of mind for the team.  That said many folk here won’t likely understand, or mind much for the additional context being added - mindset, culture, attitude (for example), are all somewhat personal/internal to the team, and hard to define as ‘right’ or ‘wrong’ with them being unique to each persons/teams style. 

I made comment last night on the concerns flagged again, and again said they’re fair and reasonable concerns and are something the team are aware of and working through. Although I would also note that, Discord with its many new people soon to be arriving. is likely not going to be the medium that will resolve such matters - fortunately as we head into the network being live, the very nature of it will mean each and every one of us can be free to run things, support devs, build apps and work with the people or teams that we enjoy and believe in - in exactly the way we want to. 

:AutonomiRed:

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1327977531541815318)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:03:34` **forthebux** said:

These are the audiences:

1. node runners (hosts) 
2. builders and developers 
3. internet users 
4. market influencers - exchanges, partners, KOLs etc
5. token holders and traders (degens)

Make up wise people might well (and likely will) fall across/into several categories and need different things experience wise

For example:

a bad experience for a token holder would be buying a token that goes down, a good one, buying a token that goes up

a developer a bad experience would be not being able to build easily and not having any clear/proven incentive to try here, versus some other platforms with other (perhaps not comparable) features, a good experience, to build with good docs and support and to attract a customer base for their proposition 

a user, a bad experience = no experience or crappy apps that don’t offer much they’re not already getting (for a price they don’t maybe care about enough) elsewhere online, a good experience an application that is incredible and different to what they can get online today (a developers holy grail) 

a node runner - good experience - easy to set up, zero cost to download, no risk dollar/pound wise more than willing to put in to say private hosting, or running from a pc, earning something that has real life value, either through utility or market demand/value. bad experience - hard to run, no value to what is being earned/can’t earn and causing performance issues.

For all audiences clearly the fundamentals of upload, download, privacy/security and permanence need to be present - table stake stuff.

For the current rollout (and to attract any more and all of the same) people need to know there is a network, know what it is doing and have ways to access it through whichever type of experience they want. Everyday internet users may never see ‘autonomi’ and just enjoy an app or two that’s running on it (for example).

Currently we are looking towards those that are okay with a little risk, some technical jumps and a ‘new world’ type opportunity - token holders and node runners cross over nicely here.

The increases activity is also good for kols who want to report on projects that people seem keen to share and care about (snowball effect) and also demonstrate that current trading volumes are not reflective of future ones based on plans which can only really start when there is a network to use.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1328031629247123496)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:12:35` **forthebux** said:

If token holders and node runners and ai project lovers find the threads in here interesting, super. If this is more a place for sign posting and help that’s also okay. For awhile it will also be our shop window and our campaign centre, sharing here along with socials matters and it means that people looking to report on and support the project have quick access to news, sentiment and a group of people who can answer their questions.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:35:58` **happybeing** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> These are the audiences:

1. node runners (hosts) 
2. builders and developers 
3. internet users 
4.... [truncated]

Thanks @Bux lots of clarity here which is very helpful. I'm noting that marketing for the time being is NOT towards people storing and downloading, or using apps based on what you say here:
> For the current rollout (and to attract any more and all of the same) people need to know there is a network, know what it is doing and have ways to access it through whichever type of experience they want. Everyday internet users may never see ‘autonomi’ and just enjoy an app or two that’s running on it (for example).
> 
> Currently we are looking towards those that are okay with a little risk, some technical jumps and a ‘new world’ type opportunity - token holders and node runners cross over nicely here.

So for now marketing is directed at people interested in the token and running nodes?

If so, that is to an extent a relief, because the experience for some in your list (2, 3) is currently going to be very poor (anything trying to use the network to store and download). We all hope that will be resolved, but we don't know how long that will take.

In the mean time, ramping up marketing will inevitably draw in people, some with influence who care very much about the experience of 2 & 3, which is after all the essential utility of the network. So I think that ramping up marketing more generally right now is still a risky approach and that getting the basic upload and download functionality working first seems more sensible.

Would you mind cross posting the marketing summary to the forum as I think it is very helpful and others will be interested to read it?

Thanks again.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `14:29:55` **forthebux** said:

“Our encrypted chunks are "information theoretically secure". This is quantum proof as well. 

From David:

"An information-theoretically secure system is one where the security cannot be broken, even with unlimited computing power and time. The security in these systems relies on mathematical principles and is fundamentally unbreakable, unlike computational security, which depends on the difficulty of certain mathematical problems (like factoring large primes in RSA encryption) that might be solvable with enough computational resources. i.e. our chunks do not hold enough information to decode them in any way. 

So self_encrypted chunks are very secure and quantum proof.

The network itself is not so easy to quantify, as a probabilistic network and not deterministic (more like AI/LLM as opposed to programmed rules) but the approach to breaking it in many cases is just too complex."

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1329457614538674177)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `10:39:26` **forthebux** said:

Guys - it's all good, and comments are super valid, all I would say is do consider we have a few different (although they might be multiple) groups to talk to:

1. Node Runners (hosts)
2. Autonomi Network Token Holders (traders)
3. Builders and Developers (app providers/content creators)
4. Platforms (highly distributed and secure storage for their creators)
5. Internet/Data Users (attracted by apps, not likely aware of The Network)

4 will care about some of the metrics being discussed very much - this is about capability and credibility among people that may decide to get to know more (or not) in only a few secs - there's a lot of stuff out there, and some of it is very well backed and very loud.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1330124388128919654)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `10:41:02` **forthebux** said:

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:00:52` **forthebux** said:

Oh sorry, I've been dipping in and out as over a few things today - not seeing, and not acknowledging are very different. So the new emissions will go live soon, but we have a step in the interim - which samples the network to give earnings to good nodes. Now we don't want to upload random data to do this - as it would start to take space which would also affect the price of hosting - not good. So instead we are sending Attos to good nodes frequently to act as a flag. 

The amount of token emission to active nodes per day will be deterministic

The emission service will constantly sample active nodes in the network.

You can think of it as an uploader that doesn't upload data. The service generates random files and query the network for nodes close to the file addresses. The received nodes and their evm addresses are recorded. But no files are actually uploaded so that we don't flood the network (see my point above)

While the sampling happens throughout the day the actual token payment will happen with a fixed cadence.

There are 3 tuneable params, that we will be observing before committing to emission design in a few weeks:

1. Sampling frequency (how often the service is going to generate random files and query the network).
2. Random address count (how many addresses the service will generate when querying the network).
3. Node per random address (how many close the service will sample for each random address).

As you will likely deduce, the params in item 3 determine how many nodes will be sampled per day. It is desirable to sample more nodes as Autonomi grows so that even a small home node operator is able to be paid.

Noting here that while we will automatically adjust the sampling size as The Network grows, for now, in these super early stages, the process will be manual and the params will be reviewed every week.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1338993184352636958)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:07:51` **forthebux** said:

Technically the guys would be able to answer this far better than I, but from my pov - we have huge amounts of spare capacity and as devices continue to get better the capacity will grow. Our devices, which already have an impact should be used and utilised fully, right now we’re going to be building data centres galore. From a practical perspective I’m not sure how much data (when thinking cloud specifically) is ever truly deleted or removed (Apple have had such cases for example), so the idea of pay once and know it’s there (should you choose to access it again) is key to the definition of permanence - a reason why there can be no charge as well - it’s already yours, you should have to pay if that’s the case and the network is essential a shared storage layer but private drive for the world

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1341924244237582336)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:11:37` **sailingintothewind** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Technically the guys would be able to answer this far better than I, but from my pov - we have huge ... [truncated]

but but - there remains the question of why would you need to keep old data if you know it will never be used - it still uses resources even though there is spare and growing capacity out there. There is an undeniable environmental impact of keeping data which requires electricity and components. Sorry - I completely understand the permanence and ephemeral and capacity points, but as a race humans tend to use all resources until there is nothing left or things reach a tipping point - this is why I ask

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1341925191156236308)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:31:57` **forthebux** said:

@DavidL we've looked at your issue, you have not been sent your ANT as we couldn't verify the signature (might have been a mistake generating the signature, copying the values, or something). Please would you be kind enough to complete the process again and submit the form once more? Of course no need to burn again(!), you just need to sign the ETH address with your Omni wallet again, and submit the info along with the same burn transaction hash. You will then be automatically included in the next airdrop of ANT on Friday 🙏

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:20:37` **forthebux** said:

Lisa, Rusty is trying to help you -the process of the MAID burn and claim was put in place so that people from the early OMNI days didn't have to go through KYC. And because we want to give you ANT at this super early stage of the network's life. I appreciate you have likely held MAID for years and that this likely feels like a good opportunity (based on your comments) to 'get out' and so while I appreciate your urgency and frustration, the best way to address and resolve both (as much as is possible at this stage), is to follow the steps that have been outlined. The issue (not created on network contract side) with signature errors (can be a copy paste error or something else) is not ideal, but it isn't insurmountable and you will get your ANT 1:1 for MAID you have burned.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1347589765670637701)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:35:34` **forthebux** said:

Fun as it is to poke (not sure what will be achieved to the benefit of you and/or the network though). 1-4 all went out to those eligible, other than those who for leaderboard rewards, needed to attach a discord name to a wallet and haven't *yet done so. For 5, everyone who correctly provided the signature post burning has been sent their ANT, those who have encountered errors (usually due to signature not being correctly captured) have been given a process to follow in order to get their tokens sent - we of course, as I hope you would expect, don't want to send ANT to wrong addresses.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:37:15` **forthebux** said:

We’ve just got linear going as we’re working in a more joined up way going forwards - before the guys would work on their thing, but the issue is some of the decisions on one part (to fix or enhance) had implications on others - one bit maybe better but to the overall detriment of  another part or function. David and Nic are now in lock step (Nic is amazing but needs David’s technical depth and overview to really support the guys to the fullest). Qi and Benno will stay on investigations and issue/bug squashing (network and client side), Anselme, Mick and Roland are working closely as a hit squad - and they’ve already got the nice linked up working from one to another going. And Victor (our new QA), after finishing working on python api, will be working closely with Chris, Ermine and Shu. It should all come together much better as a fully operating network team, rather than a team of specialists with their own areas who don’t really follow or track/understand what is happening elsewhere. 

I will see if there’s a format (maybe linear) that can be published or maybe we open up one of our weekly calls for those that want to listen in. Let me pick up with Nic on Monday as to what would work for the guys best and I can report back on the stages on Wednesday?

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1347639251097096212)

It depends, it will be 12 months so after the first and second  Impossible Futures Programs so there will be a fair few apps up and running on the network then and if we do this well much more visibility and enthusiasm surrounding the network - we have to factor in all of the different types of people who need to participate in the ecosystem in order for it to grow, the greater the scale, the less the influence the whales have as eventually, as you point out, it will disperse overtime, providing there are willing buyers on the other side of their sales of token.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1349534934133510239)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:16:08` **forthebux** said:

A developer can monetise their app application however they wish, if they wish - what they create/build is up to them. There is an incentive to build with Autonomi beyond the principle of doing so, through the program also - after two weeks as long as the POC hits the qualifying criteria (to be refined and shared), they get $250, plus ANT. (Even if not in top 12 to progress through to qualify for next round of rewards).

If they do get voted through to the next phase (adding more value to the network as more likely to become a functioning application that requires upload of data), then they receive $500 and 1000 ANT at that stage - after which they are also in with a chance to win one of the awards on offer for their application at the end of the program.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1349536473153536133)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:20:24` **forthebux** said:

We’re working on the criteria/rules and restrictions for this now - will drop on forum tomorrow so that you guys can kick it a bit - it’s a difficult balance, as we have lots of token within our community, but little token outside of it - the idea of pay to vote is also to bring new participants in, meaning their votes have to matter, and that there needs to be an incentive to acquire ANT in order to place a vote in the first place

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1349537549101568087)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:25:28` **forthebux** said:

If there is no payment, we just open up to botting and lose an easy token sink, unlike locking, there is a slight risk in voting (as well as aligned incentives), if your project doesn’t make it through, then there is no upside as you don’t get an ANT reward for your vote and you have spent your ANT - this of course opens the - back all of them to hedge debate … good for sinking token, but creates a problem of inflation on the backend when rewards go out, but there are options and we’ve got some great builds and will ask for more before we lock into a mechanic 🤝

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1349538821687218246)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `00:33:44` **forthebux** said:

Yes, but it still has to qualify (otherwise it’s too open to exploitation), this is where the human element comes in. 

For example - if you vote and your project gets through to the next phase, but it doesn’t win anything, yes you still get the benefit of voting. But, if you vote for a project that doesn’t qualify into the next round – then you would just lose the ANT that you have used to vote. Appreciate much clarification/detail still needed - but we’re sharing early with you guys so we can build out terms and criteria more in open with you before we start pushing, and promoting next week

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:25:50` **forthebux** said:

We are using an ERC token called ANT - that is live and on exchanges, it is also earned through emissions and hosting data. 

ANT will be able to be locked up and used to support builders - the website link will give you a decent high-level overview of plan here. 

The developer (builder) test net will open on April 22 - test-nets work great, no upload errors etc as only small, circa 2k nodes - as opposed to 30M+

Today is March 14, so while work continues on with needed updates, by the network team, another group of team members, will begin building out awareness and sign-ups ahead of the program starting.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:24:53` **forthebux** said:

There is no ‘our’ people - MaidSafe and the foundation are supporting (and deploying) this optional initiative to help builders,   doing so in the best way we can. As we have said from the beginning, this is a prototype, and the first time something like this has been done on the network, there is no upside beyond supporting the network to scale, helping the builders to realise their concepts and benefiting the community who are connected (in whichever way) to a decentralised network – that we are desperately trying to get out of the way of.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:34:38` **forthebux** said:

It’s not done on purpose, I think there’s a lot of tail chasing going on with a team of two trying to ensure alls are covered and all builders are supported. There is no finger on the scale from the foundation’s or MaidSafe’s perspective at all. This is the first filter to make sure the community are able to back viable projects - as while there is no risk in losing tokens with backing, folks are locking them up for 12 months, doing that against projects with the wrong intent wouldn’t be ideal

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:35:06` **forthebux** said:

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:35:29` **forthebux** said:

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:37:44` **forthebux** said:

Indeed, but for the initial voting if we had purely selected the applications, we might be having a conversation as MaidSafe being a centralised entity that’s taking control and having its favourites - hopefully you can see how there is a very difficult balance here, opening up to community vote, to allow the Wisdom of the Crowd to find the good ones, having 12 places, was designed to make sure the community had their say

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1372493142678573198)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:38:24` **forthebux** said:

If we let all 30+ through, based on the initial criteria given, we would have a quality problem, and also as such a small team, we would struggle to support everybody as much as we can, in order to have some live applications on network

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1372493308969877564)

---

For the top 3 spots, it will be based on score - 60% of this will be derived from backing, and 40% will come from judges - there are ten judges - 2 principle (with highest weighing) - these are @dirvine and @BambooGarden, 3 specialist guest judges (who will only judge projects in their category - entertainment, infa or knowledge), and 5 community judges, that the community have nominated. 

(the form to submit nominations for community judges is on the forum)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:33:33` **forthebux** said:

I agree there needs to be a killer app, where that comes from is another question - I would rather back 1000 builders to try, than one company to own (maybe Impossible Futures as it forms is also that 'product' an automated disruptor to the VC market, where you don't give up your ownership as a builder but you have access to users and all the things you need, and there's a million dollar as well as ANT pool ...maybe). 

I made a post about some of the decentralised nature of what we're trying to do as a team, in the forum - this bit is probably quite relevant:

*Our aim ... now we can see what the reality is, has been to give the network the best possible chance of scaling success. For that to happen we not only need to establish the baseline (101 table stakes), but also the tools, materials and incentives that people will ultimately need to be able to engage with the network in the way they would like to - eventually this will have nothing to do with MaidSafe - that’s the aim.*

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1372582683376619580)

## Friday, 2025-05-16

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `02:20:40` **janeytheterrible** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> I agree there needs to be a killer app, where that comes from is another question - I would rather b... [truncated]

I still reckon the killer app is one which allows a person to create a video channel where they can upload videos, and the companion app is the one which displays the channels out there, plays the videos, etc

The apps would have to at least match what youtube provides for a channel.  But of course there is no entity controlling the channels, the channel owners add their channel to a, for want of a better name, database of channels.

The viewers can search the channel "database", add channels to their favourites in the viewer.app 

youtube is perhaps one of the most use sites involving such amounts of storage, bandwidth, and control of any company.

These 2 companion apps would destroy youtube and open up a censorship free, non-algorithm driven viewing platform out there.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1372745538318893166)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:32:52` **forthebux** said:

the summary is, now that Autonomi is live the 'red' category in the WP pie chart (those who supported MaidSafe via investing/buying shares in the company), have been provided with an allocation of ANT by the network (created at TGE). These token allowances have been sent to shareholders (or will be for those still needing to provide an address) via an NFT, with their NFT having the ability to unlock that specific individual's allocation. They can unlock and claim 50% of their ANT in May and the remaining 50% in August, they don't have to claim though (there is no expiry) and ofc once the tokens are their wallet what happens next with them and when is entirely up to them. There were also folk within the community who provided loans to MaidSafe, these have been settled via the same process (**the tokens for this are coming from The Autonomi Foundation's allocation**), and they have the same unlocking schedule. The conversation above is that some people receiving an NFT have chosen to put them up on OS for sale.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1375164528731623434)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:14:39` **forthebux** said:

Several things - the TLDR is:

You select the projects you think have the most merit (will do well) and you back them by locking ANT against them for 12 months.

In 12 months you get your ANT back and if they go live on Autonomi (hitting the criteria) by the conclusion of this Impossible Futures, then they (and you as a backer) are eligible to share in the rewards pool

If they don't then you just get the ANT you locked back 

you can find the details and also back if you would like here:

impossible-futures.com

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1382105708924895293)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:56:50` **forthebux** said:

The emissions rewards are limited (first 12 years but they fall off after the first 5 quite dramatically), and so it’s important that they help the network as it’s needing to scale, we have a lot in flight now (beyond just MaidSafe efforts also) - so while emissions will increase, the delay is intended to support token health (utility, demand etc) which impacts network (through incentives, perception etc) - data flowing onto the network at greater rate will also mean that nodes can be paid to host and not to just be there as willing capacity (just getting/relying only on emissions for their contribution as a result)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1382282418236690512)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `10:02:42` **forthebux** said:

The updates schedule (which won’t be that dramatic) and won’t extend out beyond the original will land with the roadmap, the tech and token need to work together for reasons such as increasing scale and demand re: storage

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1382283896984436768)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `10:07:07` **forthebux** said:

This is not correct - emissions amounts are set, we’re only adjust the current timeline slightly in line with my comments above, based on where we’re at in reality now live, to ensure we drive forward with the most accessible, compelling and scalable proposition we can.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1382285007556444170)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `10:16:04` **forthebux** said:

Morning! There are lots of variables, including where we are now (next to zero data demand) and where the network can be (millions of files being uploaded each day/hour through interactions with applications etc) - it’s tempting to look at something in isolation (we have fallen foul of that a few times tech wise) and tweak one thing, but doing so has implications elsewhere, sometimes unknown. Node quoting will be impacted by the demands on them, so the first place to look is not just utility and sentiment connected to Autonomi and its token (builders) but through the amount of data we can put up on the network in a more seamless/batched way (partner data)  - this coupled with the updates network side guys have started to run at now they’re deploying (or soon deploying post staging) the client network side updates feels like the best place to start - really don’t want to be putting any thumbs, even well intentioned ones like David’s on the scale, appreciate that definition is slightly different based on perspective though

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1382287261131083776)

---

## Thursday, 2025-06-12

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:58:43` **forthebux** said:

yes and no - DAVE (which will be being released with new name and updates) and can be used directly by anyone without care for the why or how re: the network (benefitting from it and caring/understanding it are two different things) ...

BUT builders will also (and more likely) attract those types of 'end users' too (specific audience within that group will of course be dependent on what they build and what business models they choose to implement). 

Builders bringing users and data to the network (which will be bring utility for ANT due to upload data fee), doesn't necessarily mean users will be aware of ANT - visibility of this i.e. what a user sees on the frontend, as opposed to what needs to happen in the backend is up to the builder of the app /UI or experience ... right now, I have no idea for the apps I currently use (DAPP included) what the project or business owners hosting costs are ... in web2, I just use the application.

For end-users and builders, we will continue to improve accessibility as well as build out Impossible Futures (and launch new look DAVE). 

For the third audience 'Partners' (those holding lots of data they want to in first instance back up), I typed a little on this yesterday when chatting with Max - but in essence I think of these folks as people like: Academic Researchers, Cultural Preservationists or Media Contributors, content creators or knowledge custodians who want to know their work product or information is secure and stored forever - without concern about third party contracts, fees or centralised overrule or service/privacy issues - they're another audience we now need to start speaking too - this is a new initiative we are hiring for and will being work on, now that we are where we are. 

I guess what I'm trying to say/type badly, is that the everyday user will come once there are apps, but that Builders can come now - providing we have the accessibility and functions that they need and deserve to be able to work with/from. 

Partners can engage with Autonomi but only once there is a more accessible way to use data uploads - we had to make a start there with the work on client network side (now in staging), and will need to continue to build the layer/tools for them while the network node code update is being worked on (we're planning on the latter being handled by Anselme and Qi with David). @Nic cc'ing you as a fair amount here that relates to our back and forth on planning, and links into our call.

## Saturday, 2025-06-14

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:39:56` **mightyfool0689** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Morning! There are lots of variables, including where we are now (next to zero data demand) and wher... [truncated]

Sorry @Bux late reply.  But I wanted to revisit this answer, didn’t have the time until now. What I do not understand is why the floor (at 0 data demand) is set as low as it is. With data being perpetual and a burden on the network I don’t think it’s ever a good idea to have uploads as cheap as they’re now. Realistically, I think we will only ever have a network that is 0% full during the start of the network, never after a few years. So even though it’s good to incentivize people/companies to jump in early at low storage prices, It should imo never be this cheap, because as mentioned, it’s still a permanent burden on the network. I fail to see what the implication’s elsewhere would be other than some early adopters not being able to fill the network at basically no (ANT) cost. Not sure if I’m missing something.

## Monday, 2025-07-07

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `15:10:15` **forthebux** said:

It's a decentralised ecosystem, that isn't up nd running yet. The volume is low, so any trade (in or out) will have an impact currently. Gatekeeping is not an answer, and tokens are with those who earned them over the last years, if they want out before the network starts that is entirely their choice, as it should be. I would rather a climb ahead than a fall, so starting at the bottom, or getting close to it is okay - there is no data being uploaded, there are no apps being flocked to by 100s/1000s of people, there is no realisation of the vision in practise (only in prophet) ... so yes, the token today reflects the reality of today, but it's the work today (and everyday) that will impact the token from here. We had to launch to see what we're seeing (R&D is a dangerous phase when you get comfy and stay in it alone) - and you should see with the updates that the impossible network, is not just possible, but useable.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `08:52:46` **forthebux** said:

It's not neglect I assure you of that, I am sorry for the delay, it's just that a change (revision to where levels should be) without the corresponding and expected level of function and demand, will not only not really effect the material value that node owners receive right now, but it will also have an impact as to how we incentivise node owners more with rewards in the future as they become more needed - i.e. the network will be needing to expand to support spikes and then increasing data uploads as we (hopefully) become the default for how data is preserved at scale online. 

Typing candidly when I first joined I asked the guys to document what the upcoming features and releases were, and as a result of that we added in complementing aspects - that, for reasons I have learned to understand more, was reflective less of reality but of aspiration. Not this time ... we have one big initiative that will continue to scale (Impossible Futures), but we also have two importantly accessible network products (to give easy access to the network on a personal use case and also a partner data use case basis) .

Along with IF props which will be going live over the summer, the network generated products, will give much more utility to the ANT token, and nodes will be able to begin being paid for storage and not be solely relying on emissions (which may seem a little nuts but is necessary at the very beginning of all this when there is zero demand but we need supply waiting in readiness to address it). 

There is a vast amount of data that partners want to upload, right now, so the focus is on ensuring they can do this in a simple, error free and cost effective way (acknowledging nodes need to be compensated and that it is only a single fee forever), as soon as possible. Once we have that we won't just have 'the vision' of the future to share for our needed  marketing and campaign aspects - we will have facts, partners and proof points outside of 'web3'. We will also have all the logic we need to get emissions back to where they should be, so that they again reflect, or at least re-meet with the WP timeline - supporting the network and rewarding those who help and enable it scale, as intended.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1392413159100256346)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `19:16:43` **forthebux** said:

you can't (unless you're a meme coin and doing degen web3 things) expect to have trading volume or demand attached to a utility token with no real utility (supply/demand ratios matter, as does the sentiment around the project those apsects are attached to). We now have enough improvements rolling out (all things David is mentioning in here) to start having data uploaded to the network - so we can have token utility.  We ofc need to look at sentiment (not speaking to the forum here but to the market more broadly) and for that we need proof not more prophecy - this is why this week was so important (release and the beginnings of partner uploads, both of which happened), and why getting the network product usable is the next key step (among other 101 elements - security being a huge one, I'm not going to use Autonomi as my backup if the network can be attacked by those not wishing for data to be safe and perpetual on the web forever). We have MM support also, but the point of fact is, all inputs need to be connected to real world outputs - which is the point I was making when I say we're in a place to address the issue, as opposed to just reacting to the symptom

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1396194056127778926)
