**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:15:43` **forthebux** said:

1. The guys are confident that despite some issues with downloads, that the data is there (permanence and upload successful), we are currently working on ‘streaming’ so that we can have ALL file sizes uploading (essential for broad use). We have an internal hackathon next week to get a jump start on a upload (for lack of a better word) gateway, so I suspect we will find and discover more issues from there to address also. Once we have this we can go a bit more into sales mode (we fall down at the ‘okay, great but how’ stage right now which is what needs to be addressed to change the trajectory).

2. The partner is private data and security, we need to have a friendly, knowing the status we’re all aware of right now, that will put real data (not junk or public but private) onto the network so that we can prove out what we’re here to do, so that others will be happier and forthcoming to do it

## Wednesday, 2025-08-13

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `19:02:34` **forthebux** said:

I think choice is important, people will gather where they like, the forum will always get the dev blog, there's a huge amount of history and discussion in there and I'm sure for many that has a lot of value. I hope that as we scale and get the feet under this thing that there will be many different developer communities that will start to form. From a MaidSafe team pov we want to create a place where new people feel enthused and welcomed and where the devs (and non devs) alike can pop in and work with folks to trouble shoot and gather insights - the forum can be a great place for that also, although at times it can (like discord without moderation) get charged to a point where it becomes at odds to (what I hope) are the aims of all that want to see Autonomi grow.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1405250195394003085)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `19:24:01` **forthebux** said:

AJ has that in hand with The Foundation, to make sure it's supported better going forward, which should get us out and keep us out of the watch zone (headline is MEXC want volume) . The key is for us to get this ready so we can deploy the IF projects on main, get DAVE finally live (retail use case but we can use in other ways to get folk coming in, after all the wayback machine gets wiped on occasion and wiki now has age verification as well as editors), and Indelible. We will run security aspects/updates a long side these spikes of effort. We will chat about it more next week but it will be so nice to finally have a way to access the (working) network more easily - the AI use case (disappearance of knowledge as we know it, or don't as the case maybe) is a huge one imo.

## Friday, 2025-08-15

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `17:04:42` **forthebux** said:

Agree - there is a lot to mention right now with so much discourse around tech enabling overreach, but it always comes back to the 'so what?'.

That is what we need to be able to actually address (rather than just talk/preach about addressing it). It feels frustrating, but it's a process we have to go through still. 

Ideas, ideals and intents can be compelling, to an extent, but it's the realisation of those things that actually matters. Jim's video (I think) highlights that. The reality of what will be built is exactly what we are (and need to continue to) work toward. The subsequent dollars spent on marketing will then be justified, as they can generate positive results and engagement with the network, either directly (degen style) or through applications (current internet user style) - hopefully at some point those worlds will also converge.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1405945306587070545)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:19:08` **morph9232** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Agree - there is a lot to mention right now with so much discourse around tech enabling overreach, b... [truncated]

In my business experience it is always easier to build the product than it is to market it. You will have your job cut out for you. There is so much on social media to compete with, so many promises, lots of disappointment, and apathy, and so much noise. On the other hand, Autonomi will have much to offer. It is the real deal. I have noticed that every once in a while a new user discovers this project, and their comments in discord are like: holy moly! I just stumbled upon Autonomi, it’s amazing, why is this not more on social media? How do I use it? How can I upload? How can I share links, etc.  Once we are ready for the world, I think the world will come, at least in certain groups, but we will still need a steady trek up the marketing mountain. Cheers

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1405964037518262485)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:17:26` **traktion0_20257** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> It's not a move yet - it's a future state we're open to, so that the token, once it starts to perfor... [truncated]

For me, it is nothing to do with on or off ramps. It is about being able to add liquidity to the network, purely for resource exchange.

What concens me is that gas fees _dwarf_ the data cost. Scratchpads are essentially as cheap as immutables, for example. Fees earned by nodes runners are tiny.

I accept that blockchain was necessary for launch, but it seems to be growing claws. This worries me.

Anyway, I'll not fuel this fire as it will swamp all other debate today. I just want to underline my concern and the feeling that it is often down played.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:49:22` **forthebux** said:

Guys, just to interject here, there appears to be (at least through my interpretation of the thread Ive just worked through) an under current that for some reason the MaidSafe team have 'misled' the community about the implementation of a native token. That is untrue, work and proposals/outlines have in fact been worked on recently by Qi - any comments related to intent and mindset/approach are subjective and based on (unless you have categoric text/talk) on assumptions and unknowns. 

We will take on the feedbacl/reaction of last night, and to be clear the status/approach currently, is that immediate term we need to get fees reduced - more on that next week, next we need to explore whether Native token is the right route (before we continue on), or if there is a frog leap in terms of tech that changes that conversation. 

Noting - it is a conversation.

I'm not gonna lie. I love David. But he makes me feel extremely stupid sometimes. It does make me wonder if David should be the one sharing on things like X spaces. He has so much technical knowledge that its difficult to follow for mainstream people. Additionally, on X there is no way to follow along visually and his accent makes it a bit more difficult to follow (for me) in general. 

Personally I think we should use the main outreach channels, like X, for mainstream. Where David will be much better for people already within our community, showcasing things while sharing a presentation, a pre-recorded video or live screen sharing. Really going a bit more slow and in-depth on a certain topic.

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `18:21:10` **forthebux** said:

Also just to be fair, and balanced. If a provider is running a large percent of the network nodes, and getting a large percent of the emissions (and selling those if they decide to), as a result that in itself, in time, will still be of net benefit to the network (assuming they’re actually providing that storage space). What we don’t want of course is everybody spinning up nodes that have no capacity, or taking  nodes constantly on and offline to try and optimise their earnings  - with the gas fee issue being addressed imminently (beyond the first step which will reduce the current fee down), any risk of network instability/data lose is not acceptable for a network of this nature.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1422996781683445942)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `11:46:57` **forthebux** said:

the network does not directly generate real users, unless you're talking builders as users. The interaction layer is the key - the products, apps etc that enable people to benefit from the network, without having to understand CLI, or indeed anything related to the infrastructure itself. The people who believe that Autonomi can be built on and used in this way can buy, hold or earn token, the token will reflect the increasing utility/growth/data that the network is attracting - having ANT/running a node should be another beneficial part of the ecosystem

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `09:20:56` **forthebux** said:

IF is a huge opportunity for the network, we have a new committee member at the foundation who is very well connected, so key for me there is to build those relationships into IF collaborations, tech, comumunities, problem area, prize funds etc. Beyond that the developer focus is important (which also plays into IF of course), but having products like Dave that can have the tech tested in them and then be pulled apart and made better by others is a good thing to promote. Indelible is an org focus, so we can actually do BD (which may lead to announcements if people don't want to use privately - the data will benefit and be felt by the network in terms of demand and node incentive anyway ofc), once you have a few aspects like that cooking (proof) then the publications and 'influncers' have something beyond vision to report on and write about - we will start with some of those titles more active in the crypto space, but Autonomi goes well beyond that, and so with a little momentum and enthusiasm behind it, the news should travel out into the real tech world too (noting the end users will likely come for the interactions they can have, not the infrastructure they're hosted on).

**[#🎙︱discussion-topics]** `22:55:59` **forthebux** said:

We’ve looked into gamification of this nature before, and there was no actual gain by doing such an action (off and on - although at this early network stage the same was a potential creator of instability) - I’m also very aware not to jump to the suspicious, big bad actor ‘whale hunt’ - big node operators are critical, particularly at this early stage of the network and its designed and okay if people are rewarded for providing large amounts of space. That said, and being mindful that this is the token channel and emissions (especially while earnings for data quoting/hosting are negligible) have a large impact,  I will have a look into this with the guys tomorrow as appreciate there is a new and open discussion related to this

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:07:20` **forthebux** said:

@Toivo In terms of priorities it comes back to the fact that capacity right now isn’t the issue, we have people operating notes, what we really need is people being able to (and wanting to), upload data

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1436099659226415256)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:08:38` **forthebux** said:

It really doesn’t matter if the network is improved, if we still don’t have anybody using it, we also can’t expect people to use it if it isn’t fit for purpose – this is where it gets tricky, and as I mentioned in the earlier post, there isn’t a perfect sequence to be had, but rather a combination of levers that we have to pull little by little to get everything done in the most sensible and optimum way – especially important with the size of the team we have

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1436099988768690177)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:09:35` **forthebux** said:

So it’s not that it’s not a priority, it’s just that we have some burning things we need to get right, and a sequence and implementation must be done correctly in order for later updates to have the best impact – building the audience in time for those impacts to be felt is also okay

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1436100226044661851)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:10:19` **forthebux** said:

As soon as we get some improvements around usability, we need to get back to improvements around some of the core functionality, to ensure we can keep the users and data flowing in

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1436100412980592852)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `21:10:57` **forthebux** said:

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1436100571529740438)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:40:25` **forthebux** said:

The issue with things like the word ‘organic’, is that it’s hard to define- there isn’t a one, two, three step sequence to this – creating the supply, to cater for the demand when it arrives, and seeing and addressing challenges associated with that, is much better than the reverse situation, where the demand is there but the supply side falls short and puts people off. I think we also put way too much emphasis on current emissions, which in truth, particularly with this early stages of the token (price as well as liquidity), and as only one of two incentives, is a little misleading – we’re looking at one issue because it feels painful, but there are much bigger wider things to tackle in order for it to no longer be of massive relevance. We also need to consider the fact that those nodes could well have the capacity they claim to have, being punitive based on a perception is not something we could do, and as mentioned last week any solution that puts a central control or mechanism in place isn’t appropriate either - you just have to get through the growing pains, it’s a little odd as the network feels old, but in fact it’s less than 12 months in age and as David says, still teeny

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438659800555589683)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:45:24` **cobite.** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> The issue with things like the word ‘organic’, is that it’s hard to define- there isn’t a one, two, ... [truncated]

But wouldn't it be better to wait for incentivizing users that provide the supply, until the network is actually ready? The network is still in production and would have been good to see how it worked with being able to provide storage for the ant token, without additional incentives. With the incentives in place it creates a situation where the price will continue to go down until we get real amounts of data on the network, which might not be for some time until the fixes are in place. And with the price going down it makes it look like a failed project to potential users & companies. 

I just feel this has kind of been an engineered doom-loop because we have unleashed incentives for an unfinished network.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438661054199500843)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:45:33` **forthebux** said:

Was speaking with the guys on this today, we have started to now have a taste of what live development is really like – it’s not all about incentives, sometimes it’s purely for the thrill of doing something/breaking something/beating something, people on the Internet do odd things – you’ve only got to look at social media and trolling to see that. Trying to second-guess a group, or even an individual, rather than focusing on security as a dynamic need (especially with ai), feels like the best move and use of resource right now (as you can see from the update, we’re working on fair amount with a small, yet perfectly formed … team)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438661094624460820)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:46:27` **forthebux** said:

The network won’t be ready, if we just always wait until the network is ready – we won’t see exploits until there’s a reason to exploit the network, we won’t see data until there is a service that can host it, we won’t see nodes wanting to run and earn rewards, unless there is a token in market – like I say there is no one, two, three step

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438661319766179974)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:49:44` **mightyfool0689** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> The issue with things like the word ‘organic’, is that it’s hard to define- there isn’t a one, two, ... [truncated]

What I personally would like to see is a simple survey every now and then among the community. I think it would be great for the team to get some valuable insights on the state of the network. It could just be a simple:

- are you running nodes 
- are you using antctl, launchpad, other
- how many nodes do you run
- how much effective storage capacity do they have
- at what cpu/ram/up limits are you running

I think having this information would be of great value and could identify risks. The team can then use this to a) improve awareness, b) commit more development effort to certain products that are used more / need more attention and c) have awareness campaigns among community to improve network health / limit risks. 

It would take away all those assumptions we’re making and it does not seem to take too much time and can be done by someone outside of the dev team.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438662147323203694)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:51:49` **forthebux** said:

It’s really difficult to talk specific use cases like this, as in how (and why) would we police copyright free uploads, on a secure, anonymous and private network? – node owners need to be rewarded for data payments, but if data isn’t being uploaded there is no reason to run a node and we’re back to test net size service, where we’re not really seeing anything or experiencing anything in terms of live network performance – we need something to start the flywheel, having capacity (supply) is the easier than having demand – citing a few individual use cases of data upload on an anonymous basis to generate further demand is going to be a difficult hook to use for bigger data generators and owners

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438662669212319764)

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `22:51:52` **janeytheterrible** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> Was speaking with the guys on this today, we have started to now have a taste of what live developme... [truncated]

Well for the largest of the node runnings they have exclaimed many times it is all about the emissions.  Making emissions more dynamic like relating to average records stored on a node would encourage them to run an appropriate number of nodes to keep that average higher.  Maybe 40% of emissions as a base and when 100 records storage on average per node then 60% and 200 records 75% and 300 records on average 90% and 100% when its 400 records on average.

More healthy nodes and the few big players who are clear that it is all about emissions and not abuse will reduce slightly to keep emissions higher.  White paper levels will happen quick enough.

Please also stop with the 100% or 0% emissons as this is not what is being suggested.  But a dynamic system that encourages more data stored on nodes.  But not too agressive.  This way we get higher data per node with lots of nodes.  Node health, security in nimbers still, less %age ran by the big player who are clear its all about emissions

## Friday, 2025-11-14

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:13:04` **davidmc0** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> The issue with things like the word ‘organic’, is that it’s hard to define- there isn’t a one, two, ... [truncated]

I really appreciating the team prioritising engaging with the community.

"...there isn’t a one, two, three step sequence to this – creating the supply, to cater for the demand when it arrives, and seeing and addressing challenges associated with that, is much better than the reverse situation, where the demand is there but the supply side falls short and puts people off."

Highly responsive supply seems clear from the experience of millions of nodes appearing from a small community in response to emissions. I can't see why node operators wouldn't also respond rapidly to increases in data payments when demand increases.

The responsiveness of supply makes the scenario of demand outstripping supply seem unrealistic. It was a possibility prior to launch, and a key reason given for emissions in the whitepaper, but it has been shown not match with the reality we've observed on the live network with emissions.

On another note, I like the new weekly update format! Have a great weekend 🙂

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1438924709709353063)

I’m sick of it. It’s all centralised, so controlled, so disempowering, from Nvidia‘s circular money printer, to the old institutions and elites, that will find any way to pull the strings for and remove the power from the rest of us. We need something different, we need a digital infrastructure that can’t be manipulated, one that enables us all to live in the connected way we want - without having to disclose and compromise every element of who we are. It’s time for a sentiment shift, and for a new technology to be unleashed on the world.

When we succeed, targeting one point will not be enough, this is why the early stages that we’re in right now are so important, the attempted exploits will increase as the size of the network does, and the value of the data that is stored on it increases, but as long as we keep up with that (and we will have more resources as a community to be able to), then it won’t be able to be switched off – this is why there are many comments in regards to the native token, if we are using a token that has utility and on an EVM, it’s very hard to legally pursue something, when MaidSafe has built and release technology within the rules and the foundation doesn’t have any power, knowledge or influence over the data on it

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `16:44:41` **dirvine.** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> please just understand that sometime (imo) ongoing/repetitive negativity (beyond the factual/constru... [truncated]

I think it's fine to defend against bullying, condescension, and lying when it's a team forum like this. It's not a public forum for anybody for any reason. 

If it was, then full free open speech, 4chan and all that. But here it's the team trying to engage with the community. 

I think the rules are fine, and if people want to engage with the team, the bare minimum that they should be able to request is that they treat the team with respect and they demand respect from the team. I think that's not unreasonable. So it's not a free speech issue, it's not a free speech system, it's basically the team and their own Discord to communicate with the community and, most importantly, to get critique and feedback and try and see if we can just make this a better network, which is ultimately what we all want. With that I'm never gonna stand by and let people be bullied, or lied to, or spoken down to. Certainly not in a forum that has got a specific purpose like this one.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1443643709504884868)

The improvements keep rolling (2x releases pre-christmas) mean we will be able to be in a great place for 2026, and we intend to be able to state data permeance in the not too distant future. there has been a lot of work done for bad nodes, ensuring peers are able to check on another, and also not just quote for hosting but when checked by peers, have chunks they're meant to have - all of this is necessary for permanence. **[#🌐︱general-chat]** `17:54:14` **dirvine.** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> https://tenor.com/view/your-tactics-dont-work-on-me-eric-cartman-south-park-s14e9-its-a-jersey-thing... [truncated]

I think that's something that is quite good to know. When I was not very well, it was a little bit not good, actually. I didn't let anyone visit me because I didn't want anybody saying, "I hope," or "maybe," or "possibly," or "I think it'll be okay," or anything like that. 

The only way I got through what I had to get through was being 100% positive: we're doing it, and we're going to pull through. I see the network the same way. I'm not interested in "maybe it'll work," "possibly it'll be okay," or "maybe this time." All I want is: this is definitely working, and there's no doubt about it. 

That's why I don't really interact with the community much. I don't want any negativity. Beyond that, I'm a person who can do pretty amazing things with 100% positivity. I think it's a good way to go. 

We're all different, and some people have to doubt (and that's fine), but we shouldn't expect that people who doubt interact with the likes of me because it would weaken me.

So, in my position, it's 100% happening, and that's it. There is no question; it's just happening. This is how you make miracles happen. Doubt is how you don't.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1474101792509001904)

## Friday, 2026-02-20

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `12:04:09` **forthebux** said:

There are different ways to participate in the network – if you mean helping to run the network by hosting data, I am assuming, and I'm somewhat vindicated by the low node count currently, that folks will do something if it's easy and if they can earn something of value. 

Many people will still have absolutely zero interest in the technology stack behind/underneath whatever experience they are engaging in - or what's behind the features and the safeguards of those experiences (an overview is given in the full post from yesterday), but those same benefits are never the less inherently provided .

Circling back to your question, this will be to the benefit of people running nodes, who are in turn providing the invisible infrastructure and it's ongoing scale - some folks will continue to run those themselves manually, but I suspect that, in the not too distant future, most will be set up autonomously - through agents - without need for human interventions or  user flow frustrations.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1474376075650924555)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `12:09:09` **forthebux** said:

As the utility of the network increases, the utility of the token increases, meaning the visibility will also increase, and the uptake will also increase -  the incentive for buying, holding and using token increases – the value derived from running nodes is connected to all of that

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1474377337310286055)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `12:09:34` **forthebux** said:

This is the logic that I'm operating under

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1474377439374475357)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `12:17:32` **forthebux** said:

Value perception is often a reflection of effort/investment that is required to attain the value in the first place – something humming happily in the background that earns you something without any hassle, is a relatively easy prop, especially when you're only dealing with logic (Agent)

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1474379444306186432)

---

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `12:18:17` **forthebux** said:

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1474379634379591740)

> *forthebux said:*
> Remember, storage is a byproduct of usage – the focus needs to be on the latter, which is why we're ... [truncated]

I think that it all hinges on how well the value the users pay for flows to the hosting providers without shrinking on the way. At the moment the overhead of fees can only be minimized if the data batches are large. Do you have plans to make smaller data batches viable too, or are you targeting use cases with large data batches?

**[#🌐︱general-chat]** `08:53:08` **mightyfool0689** replied to forthebux:

> *forthebux said:*
> https://tenor.com/view/lovin%27-the-confidence-jedi-bob-lego-star-wars-rebuild-the-galaxy-i-love-tha... [truncated]

Hey Bux, I really don’t want to sound like a broken record, and this is gonna be the last time I’m going to address it. But looking at the current quote mechanism, I’m pretty confident something is not right. We’ve seen nodes between 8000 (50% usage)  and some 15000 (95% usage) chunks stored, and the cost for uploading is still only around 0.001 ANT or in some cases 0.005. That means uploading 1 GB of data is going to cost somewhere around $0.01 and $0.05 in ANT while it still cost around $0.12 in ETH fees.

I’m not even sure if this requires a big adjustment or if it’s something as stupid as the ANT oracle price not working as intended or something. But I really think it’s something worth looking at before launching 2.0.

[View in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1209059621319221268/1209059622586163272/1476502332706394193)
